title: Table 3600. Consumer units of one person by age of reference person: Average annual expenditures and characteristics, Consumer Expenditure Surveys, 2021-2022
unit: USD per consumer unit per year
summary: Direct near-prototype table for age 25-34 one-person consumer units.
comparability_notes: Population means rather than medians; includes some non-employed units; pension and Social Security are included as expenditures.

title: Consumer Expenditure Surveys Tables Getting Started Guide
indicator: table units conditional means and derivations
summary: Explains CE table group definitions and how to derive conditional means from aggregate means and participation shares.
comparability_notes: Derived conditional values remain means and inherit survey sampling and classification limitations.

title: Nearly Two-Thirds of U.S. Households are Family Households
summary: Provides current living-arrangement estimate for adults age 25-34.
comparability_notes: Not restricted to single employed adults and does not value in-kind housing support.

title: Economic Well-Being of U.S. Households in 2025
author_or_institution: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System
topic: young adult family support and liquidity
indicator: outside-household help housing help emergency savings
summary: Measures family help and emergency preparedness among young adults.
comparability_notes: Age band differs from the 25-34 archetype; respondent-level self-report is not a full household account.

title: Housing Prices, Inter-generational Co-residence, and Excess Savings by the Young
author_or_institution: Mark R. Rosenzweig and Junsen Zhang
topic: house prices co-residence and youth saving
summary: Studies how housing prices affect intergenerational co-residence and saving by young people.
comparability_notes: Mechanism and counterfactual evidence; not a current nationwide saving-rate estimate.

title: Personal Income in Australia, 2022-23
indicator: total personal income by age and sex
summary: Provides median personal income for the target age band.
comparability_notes: Not after-tax household disposable income and not restricted to single childless workers.

title: Housing: Census
indicator: owned outright owned with mortgage rented
comparability_notes: All ages and household types; cannot represent the youth archetype directly.

title: Young Australians living with parents: free and pay board as popular housing tenure choices
indicator: housing tenure parental co-residence and board
summary: Uses HILDA to distinguish renting, ownership, rent-free parental co-residence and paying board.
comparability_notes: Age range is wider than 25-34 and the reference year is older; not restricted to single employed adults.

title: 2025年居民收入和消费支出情况
summary: Provides current national and urban-rural aggregate calibration.
comparability_notes: National per-capita aggregates cannot be assigned to the young single archetype.

title: 2021年中国家庭金融调查数据公开发布
author_or_institution: 中国家庭金融调查与研究中心
indicator: employment income expenditure housing assets debt and social security
summary: Identifies the microdata source capable of constructing the China archetype.
comparability_notes: Actual estimates require data access codebook variable mapping weights and missing-data treatment.
